summary: Ukraine conducted strikes on Russian infrastructure including an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, port facilities in Kronstadt, and a weapons components plant in Tambov Oblast. Russian forces intensified pressure on Kostiantynivka in Donetsk Oblast using banned phosphorus munitions and guided bombs, while also striking civilian areas in Kramatorsk and Kharkiv. NATO Secretary General Rutte visited Kyiv and warned young Russians about catastrophic losses, while Zelensky stated readiness for direct talks with Putin.
key_points: Ukrainian forces struck oil terminal in St. Petersburg, port infrastructure in Kronstadt, and Progress plant in Tambov Oblast producing precision weapon components (Source: @UkraineNow/81642)
Russian forces used banned phosphorus munitions on Kostiantynivka residential quarters while continuing infantry infiltration attempts from multiple directions (Source: @UkraineNow/81643)
Two strikes hit Kramatorsk killing three and wounding four; strikes also hit Kharkiv's Kholodnohirskyi and Osnianskyi districts wounding six (Source: @UkraineNow/81650)
Enemy maintained active pressure on Kostiantynivka with infiltration attempts from south of Illinka and eastern side near Stupochky and Predtechenka (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)
Zelensky stated Russia can produce approximately 120 ballistic missiles monthly, enabling several large-scale attacks per month (Source: @UkraineNow/81652)
NATO Secretary General Rutte from Kyiv warned young Russians of significant losses exceeding 30,000 monthly, surpassing Soviet losses in Afghanistan (Source: @UkraineNow/81649)

Multiple drone threats reported across Chernihiv, Sumy, Zhytomyr, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson, Dnipro, and Odesa regions with various UAV types approaching from different directions (Source: @eRadarrua/95242)
Zelensky expressed readiness for direct talks with Putin but noted Ukraine is not current US priority due to Iran situation (Source: @UkraineNow/81648)

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine conducted significant strikes on Russian rear infrastructure including the St. Petersburg oil terminal, Kronstadt port facilities, and the Progress weapons plant in Tambov Oblast. Russian forces intensified offensive operations on Kostiantynivka using banned phosphorus munitions and continued civilian area strikes on Kramatorsk and Kharkiv. NATO leadership visited Kyiv as Zelensky warned of Russia's capacity to produce 120 ballistic missiles monthly while expressing readiness for direct negotiations.

points: Ukrainian Defense Forces struck a major oil terminal in St. Petersburg, one of Russia's key petroleum product transshipment facilities in the Baltic region, with the attack coinciding with the start of an economic forum where Putin was scheduled to speak about economic successes (Source: @UkraineNow/81638)

Ukrainian forces hit port infrastructure and ships in Kronstadt, the Progress plant in Tambov Oblast producing precision weapon components, and stopped oil pumping at the Zenzevatka station in Volgograd Oblast; other targets included Saki airfield in occupied Crimea, ammunition depots, fuel storage, and a Russian command post (Source: @UkraineNow/81642)

President Zelensky stated Russia can produce approximately 120 ballistic missiles monthly during his address to the Ukraine-NATO Council meeting in Kyiv, warning this enables several large-scale attacks per month in addition to ongoing missile terror (Source: @UkraineNow/81652)

Zelensky expressed readiness for direct talks with Putin to end the war but acknowledged Ukraine is not the current US priority, stating Iran is question number one and Ukraine is in the queue of conflicts (Source: @UkraineNow/81648)
Intelligence indicates a possible repeat of the previous night's massive attack, with Zelensky urging citizens to pay attention to air raid alerts (Source: @UkraineNow/81634)

Multiple drone threats were tracked across northern and southern Ukraine including Chernihiv, Sumy, Zhytomyr, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson, Dnipro, and Odesa regions with various UAV types including jet-powered drones approaching from Belarus and the Black Sea (Source: @eRadarrua/95242)

Greek authorities sent a protest note to Kyiv after detecting a Ukrainian unmanned surface vessel off Lefkada, warning that extending military action to the Mediterranean threatens Greek national security and maritime navigation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30652)

Russian forces conducted two strikes on Kramatorsk hitting residential buildings, killing three people and wounding four, with search and rescue operations ongoing and casualty figures being updated by Donetsk Regional Military Administration head Vadym Filashkin (Source: @UkraineNow/81650)

Russian strikes hit Kharkiv's Kholodnohirskyi and Osnianskyi districts with six people requiring medical assistance; four suffered blast injuries and glass wounds while two experienced acute stress reactions (Source: @UkraineNow/81641)

Russian forces used banned white phosphorus munitions on Kostiantynivka residential quarters with dozens of incendiary elements falling on the city, causing large-scale fires as white phosphorus ignites on contact with oxygen at extremely high temperatures (Source: @UkraineNow/81643)

Russian forces struck Dnipro hitting a retail chain warehouse with six people requiring medical help, five hospitalized including three in serious condition; Dnipro mayor Filatov criticized the asymmetry of Ukrainian military strikes versus Russian attacks on civilian infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81646)
Russian forces detected and destroyed a truck carrying a Ukrainian UAV launcher in the SMO zone using a Geranium kamikaze drone (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30650)
A bus was attacked in Yenakiieve in the DNR, with Putin being briefed on the incident according to Peskov (Source: @tass_agency/378863)
Russia presented the Argus UAV at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, capable of reaching 25 km altitude and staying airborne for 40 days using solar energy (Source: @tass_agency/378857)

Russian forces continued active pressure on Kostiantynivka with the red zone expanding south of Illinka where the enemy is persistently pushing to increase territorial control around the city; infiltration zones into the city are also growing (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

Russian forces are conducting infiltration operations on the eastern side of Kostiantynivka near Stupochky and Predtechenka, specifically attempting to capture Novodmitrivka to enable further infiltration; capturing Illinka and Novodmitrivka threatens the northern part of the city (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

Russian forces are building up advantage on the Kostiantynivka axis, reinforcing it with constant KAB guided bomb strikes on the city; defenders report active enemy drone operations and continuous Russian infantry pressure (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

A critical shortage of personnel is affecting units defending Kostiantynivka, with the Commander-in-Chief reportedly reducing infantry allocation to brigades under a fair distribution rule limiting it to only 50 people per month to form new brigades alongside existing understaffed ones (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

One brigade reportedly lost half its personnel to desertion due to inadequate command treatment, with remaining personnel including combat officers assigned to non-combat tasks like painting fences and mowing lawns while assault regiments have 12-15 battalions at their disposal (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

Multiple drone threats tracked across Chernihiv Oblast with new UAV groups entering the region, including three past Slavutich toward the Chornobyl Exclusion Zone, three toward Ripky, two toward Snovsk, and one near Mena (Source: @eRadarrua/95186)

Enemy drones detected on the border of Chernihiv Oblast and Belarus heading south, with additional drones tracked in Chernihiv and Zhytomyr regions on the Belarusian border heading south/west (Source: @kpszsu/63765)

Sumy Oblast received new drone groups from Kursk Oblast with three heading toward Buryn and one jet-powered drone taking a course toward Poltava Oblast; another jet-powered drone was tracked toward Mykolaivka in Sumy Oblast from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/95234)
A jet-powered UAV was tracked heading toward Kharkiv from the southern direction, while another jet-powered drone was reported toward Pisochyn in Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/63748)
High threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryann Oblast was reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)
Zaporizhzhia Oblast reported two drones toward Vilnyansk and one toward Novomykolaivka (Source: @eRadarrua/95237)
Two controlled UAVs were detected over the sea in Kherson Oblast with one strike drone group (Source: @eRadarrua/95240)
Enemy drones were tracked heading toward Odesa from the Black Sea (Source: @kpszsu/63783)
Active enemy tactical aviation was reported on the northern and southeastern axes (Source: @kpszsu/63767)

Ukraine faced a massive overnight attack with intelligence warning of a possible repeat attack the following night, prompting Zelensky to urge citizens to monitor air raid alerts closely (Source: @UkraineNow/81637)

Multiple drone types were deployed against Ukraine including jet-powered UAVs, standard drones, and controlled UAVs approaching from Belarus, Russian territory, and the Black Sea across numerous oblasts (Source: @eRadarrua/95242)
High threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk Oblast was declared (Source: @kpszsu/63769)
Active Russian tactical aviation operations were reported on the northern and southeastern axes (Source: @kpszsu/63767)
Russian forces used KAB guided bombs extensively on Kostiantynivka while maintaining continuous drone operations over the city (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

key_developments: Ukrainian forces conducted a significant strike campaign against Russian rear infrastructure, hitting the St. Petersburg oil terminal, Kronstadt port facilities and ships, the Progress weapons components plant in Tambov Oblast, and stopping oil pumping at Zenzevatka station in Volgograd Oblast (Source: @UkraineNow/81642)

Russian forces intensified offensive operations on Kostiantynivka using banned white phosphorus munitions on residential areas while conducting multi-directional infantry infiltration attempts from south of Illinka and eastern approaches near Stupochky and Predtechenka (Source: @UkraineNow/81643)

Russian strikes on civilian infrastructure killed three and wounded four in Kramatorsk, wounded six in Kharkiv, and wounded six in Dnipro where a retail warehouse was hit, demonstrating continued targeting of non-military objectives (Source: @UkraineNow/81650)

President Zelensky warned that Russia can produce approximately 120 ballistic missiles monthly, enabling several large-scale mass attacks per month in addition to ongoing missile terror, during his address to the Ukraine-NATO Council (Source: @UkraineNow/81652)

Critical personnel shortages are affecting Ukrainian units defending Kostiantynivka with infantry allocation reportedly reduced to 50 persons per month per brigade under a fair distribution rule, while one brigade lost half its personnel to desertion (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547)

NATO Secretary General Rutte visited Kyiv and warned young Russians that monthly losses exceed 30,000, surpassing total Soviet losses in ten years of the Afghanistan war, while Zelensky expressed readiness for direct talks with Putin (Source: @UkraineNow/81649)

Multiple drone threats were tracked across Ukraine's northern and southern regions with UAVs approaching from Belarus, Russian territory including Kursk Oblast, and the Black Sea, while high Iskander-M ballistic missile launch threat was declared from Bryansk Oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/95242)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational situation shows escalation on multiple axes with Ukraine conducting deep strikes against Russian rear infrastructure including energy and weapons production facilities, while Russia intensifies offensive pressure on Kostiantynivka using banned phosphorus munitions and maintains strikes on civilian targets. The combination of Russia's reported capacity to produce 120 ballistic missiles monthly and active tactical aviation operations indicates sustained high-intensity aerial threats. Personnel shortages in Ukrainian defending units on the Kostiantynivka axis represent a critical vulnerability that Russian forces are actively exploiting through multi-directional infiltration tactics.
sections.regional_response: NATO Secretary General Rutte's visit to Kyiv and address to young Russians signals continued Alliance political support, while the creation of the ARES expert council with retired NATO military leaders indicates ongoing efforts to reform Ukrainian forces. Greece's protest note regarding a Ukrainian vessel near Lefkada suggests potential friction over Ukrainian operations extending beyond the immediate conflict zone. The reported reduction in Ukrainian military pay increases and TCC reform concerns indicate domestic challenges affecting military sustainability.
sections.intelligence_notes: The concentration of drone threats across Chernihiv, Sumy, Zhytomyr, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson, Dnipro, and Odesa regions from multiple directions suggests coordinated Russian UAV operations targeting both northern and southern Ukraine. The use of jet-powered drones alongside standard UAVs indicates evolving Russian drone tactics. Reports of personnel issues including desertion, inadequate command treatment, and non-combat task assignments highlight systemic challenges in Ukrainian force management that may affect defensive capabilities on critical axes like Kostiantynivka.
